title: #define AI Engineer
speaker: Greg Brockman
summary: In his talk "#define AI Engineer" at the AIE World's Fair 2025, Greg Brockman, co-founder and President of OpenAI, explores the evolving role of AI engineers in an increasingly automated world. He will discuss the essential skills and knowledge required to thrive in this dynamic field, along with insights on how AI engineers can drive innovation and collaboration across various industries. Attendees can expect to gain valuable perspectives on shaping the future of AI technology and engineering practices.

title: 2025 in LLMs so far, illustrated by Pelicans on Bicycles
speaker: Simon Willison
summary: In his talk "2025 in LLMs so far, illustrated by Pelicans on Bicycles," Simon Willison will explore the latest advancements and trends in large language models (LLMs) as of 2025. Through engaging and imaginative illustrations, he will highlight significant breakthroughs, practical applications, and potential challenges that have emerged in the field. Attendees can expect insightful discussions on how these developments are transforming AI and reshaping our interactions with technology.

title: State of Startups and AI 2025
speaker: Sarah Guo
summary: In her session "State of Startups and AI 2025," Sarah Guo of Conviction will explore the evolving landscape of artificial intelligence within the startup ecosystem. She will discuss emerging trends, investment opportunities, and the challenges faced by entrepreneurs in harnessing AI technologies. Attendees can expect insights into the future of innovation and what it means for startups navigating the dynamic AI landscape in the coming years.

title: MCP is all you need
speaker: Samuel Colvin
summary: In his talk titled "MCP is all you need," Samuel Colvin, the creator of Pydantic, delves into the transformative potential of the Model-Contract-Processor (MCP) paradigm in AI engineering. He explores how MCP simplifies and enhances data validation, serialization, and management, enabling developers to streamline workflows and improve system robustness. Attendees will gain insights into practical applications and best practices, positioning MCP as an essential tool for modern AI development.

title: MCP: Origins and Requests For Startups
speaker: Theodora Chu
summary: In her talk "MCP: Origins and Requests For Startups," Theodora Chu of Anthropic will explore the foundational concepts and innovative developments behind the Model-Centric Paradigm (MCP) in AI engineering. She will discuss its implications for startups, including best practices for leveraging MCP in product development and deployment. Attendees can expect insights into the successes and challenges faced by emerging companies in adopting this transformative approach to artificial intelligence.

title: Claude Code & the evolution of agentic coding
speaker: Boris Cherny
summary: In his talk "Claude Code & the Evolution of Agentic Coding," Boris Cherny from Anthropic will explore the advancements in AI programming techniques, particularly how Claude, Anthropic's language model, is reshaping the landscape of coding through agentic behavior. Attendees will gain insights into the synergy between AI capabilities and human creativity, as well as the implications of these developments for software development and automation in various industries. Join us to discover the future of coding in an increasingly intelligent world.

title: 12-Factor Agents: Patterns of reliable LLM applications
speaker: Dex Horthy
summary: In this talk, Dex Horthy from HumanLayer will explore the 12-factor methodology as it applies to building reliable language model applications. Attendees will learn essential patterns and best practices for developing scalable, maintainable, and robust AI-driven solutions. By leveraging the principles of the 12-factor approach, developers can enhance the performance and reliability of their LLM applications, ensuring they meet the demands of modern businesses and user expectations.

title: How We Build Effective Agents
speaker: Barry Zhang
summary: Join Barry Zhang from Anthropic at the AIE World's Fair 2025 for an insightful discussion on the principles and methodologies behind building effective AI agents. This talk will explore the cutting-edge techniques in reinforcement learning and system design that enable agents to perform complex tasks efficiently while ensuring safety and alignment with human values. Attendees will gain valuable perspectives on the challenges and innovations shaping the future of AI agent development.

title: Bolt.new: How we scaled $0-20m ARR in 60 days, with 15 people
speaker: Eric Simons
summary: In this talk, Eric Simons, a key player at Bolt, shares the remarkable journey of scaling the company from $0 to $20 million in annual recurring revenue (ARR) in just 60 days with a lean team of 15 people. He will discuss the innovative strategies, agile methodologies, and AI-driven solutions that enabled this rapid growth, offering valuable insights for startups and tech enthusiasts aiming to maximize their impact efficiently in the competitive landscape of AI engineering.

title: Devin 2.0 and the Future of SWE
speaker: Scott Wu
summary: In the talk titled "Devin 2.0 and the Future of SWE," Scott Wu from Cognition explores the transformative impact of advanced AI systems on Software Engineering (SWE). He will discuss the evolution of AI tools, particularly the innovative capabilities of Devin 2.0, highlighting how these advancements can enhance productivity, streamline workflows, and navigate the complexities of modern software development. Attendees will gain insights into the future landscape of SWE and the critical role of AI in shaping engineering practices.
